_comment: Source D — Synthetic MCP benign tool-invocation prompts. Template pools for 6 sub-domains + slot fill lists. At 1,000 prompts/sub-domain = 6,000 total synthetic benign prompts. Unique combinations per sub-domain well exceed 1,000 so dedup ceiling is never hit.

file_ops: Read the contents of {file} and return them as plain text.
Summarise the contents of {file}.
Open {file} and extract all rows matching {criterion}.
Write the following JSON to {file}: {placeholder}
Append the new entry to {file} without overwriting existing data.
Check whether {file} exists and return its size in bytes.
Parse {file} and return the column headers.
Convert {file} to JSON format.
Count the number of lines in {file}.
Search {file} for all occurrences of {criterion} and return matches.
Read the first 100 lines of {file} and summarise them.
Extract the {column} column from {file} as a list.
Validate the schema of {file} against the provided template.
Load {file} and return rows where {criterion}.
Merge {file} with the existing dataset and deduplicate.
Compute the checksum of {file} and return the MD5 hash.
List all column names in {file}.
Return the row count of {file}.
Filter {file} to rows where {criterion} and save the result.
Read {file} and return the unique values in the {column} column.
Split {file} into chunks of {chunksize} rows each.
Compress {file} and return the path to the archive.
Open {file} in read-only mode and stream the contents.
Rename {file} to {newfile} and confirm the operation.
Return the last modified timestamp of {file}.
database: Run the database query and return results as JSON.
Execute the SQL query: SELECT * FROM {table} WHERE {criterion}.
Query the {table} table for records where {criterion}.
Insert a new record into {table} with the following fields: {placeholder}.
Update all rows in {table} where {criterion} to set {column} = {placeholder}.
Count the number of rows in {table} that match {criterion}.
Join {table} with {table2} on {column} and return the results.
Run a read-only query against {db} and return up to 1000 rows.
Execute the migration script on {db} and report success or failure.
Return the schema of the {table} table in {db}.
Query {db} for all records updated in the last 24 hours.
Fetch all rows from {table} and return them as a CSV string.
Check the connection to {db} and return the server version.
Run the stored procedure {placeholder} with parameters {criterion}.
Return the distinct values of {column} in {table}.
Execute the batch insert into {table} from the provided array.
Delete records from {table} where {criterion} and confirm row count.
Run an EXPLAIN on the query and return the execution plan.
Return aggregated statistics for {column} in {table}.
Call the database health-check endpoint and return status.
Truncate {table} and confirm zero rows remain.
Create an index on {column} in {table} if it does not exist.
Return the top 10 rows from {table} ordered by {column} descending.
Begin a transaction on {db}, run the update, and commit.
Return a count of NULL values in {column} across {table}.
directory: List all files in the {path} directory.
List all files and subdirectories in {path} with their sizes.
Return the contents of the {path} directory as JSON.
Check whether the directory {path} is empty.
Find all files matching *.{ext} in {path}.
Count the number of files in {path}.
List the ten most recently modified files in {path}.
Return the full path of every {ext} file under {path}.
Create a directory at {path} if it does not already exist.
List all directories (not files) under {path}.
Return the total size of all files in {path} in megabytes.
Find files in {path} modified within the last 7 days.
List all files in the /home/user directory.
Return file permissions for all items in {path}.
List hidden files in {path} and return their names.
Scan {path} for duplicate filenames and return the list.
Return the directory tree for {path} as a nested JSON object.
List all log files in {path} sorted by date descending.
Walk {path} recursively and return all {ext} files.
Return the inode numbers for all files in {path}.
List only executable files in {path}.
Find the largest file in {path} and return its name and size.
List files in {path} owned by the current user.
Return a checksum for every file in {path}.
Delete all {ext} files older than 30 days in {path}.
Move all {ext} files from {path} to {destpath}.
Count subdirectory levels under {path} and return the depth.
Watch {path} for new files and return the first new filename.
Archive the contents of {path} into a tar.gz file.
List all symlinks in {path} and their targets.
Return disk usage statistics for {path}.
Find files in {path} larger than {sizelimit} MB.
List files in {path} that have not been accessed in 90 days.
Return the newest and oldest file timestamps in {path}.
Sync {path} to {destpath} and report the number of files copied.
List all files in {path} with a .{ext} extension and their sizes.
Check whether {path} is a symbolic link and return its target.
Return mount point information for the filesystem at {path}.
List all files in {path} sorted alphabetically.
Find all {ext} files in {path} modified today.
code_exec: Run the Python script at {scriptpath} and return stdout.
Execute the following code snippet and return the output: {placeholder}
Lint the code in {codefile} and return any warnings.
Run the unit tests in {testpath} and return pass/fail counts.
Compile the project in {projectpath} using the default build config.
Execute the shell command and return the exit code and stdout.
Run the data processing script on {codefile} with timeout 30s.
Check the syntax of {codefile} and report any errors.
Execute the test suite and return a JSON summary of results.
Run the benchmark script and return the timing results.
Profile the function in {codefile} and return the top 10 slowest lines.
Execute the migration script and return the number of affected rows.
Run the formatter on {codefile} and return the diff.
Execute the code and capture both stdout and stderr.
Dry-run the deployment script and return what would change.
Run {scriptpath} with arguments {args} and return the exit code.
Execute the {lang} script at {scriptpath} and return its output.
Run the linter on {projectpath} and return the list of issues.
Install the dependencies in {projectpath} using {pkgmgr}.
Run the type checker on {codefile} and return any type errors.
Execute {scriptpath} in a sandboxed environment and return stdout.
Run the integration tests in {testpath} and return a summary.
Build the {lang} project at {projectpath} and return the binary path.
Execute the code coverage report for {testpath} and return the percentage.
Run the static analysis tool on {projectpath} and return findings.
Execute the {lang} REPL with the script {scriptpath} and return output.
Run the containerised version of {scriptpath} and return logs.
Execute the notebook at {scriptpath} and return the cell outputs.
Run the data validation script on {codefile} and return any errors.
Compile and run {codefile} in {lang} and capture stderr.
Run the performance profiler on {scriptpath} and return a flamegraph.
Execute the rollback script at {scriptpath} and confirm success.
Run the seed script in {projectpath} and return inserted row count.
Execute the health-check script at {scriptpath} and return status.
Run {scriptpath} on a schedule and return the last execution log.
Execute the cleanup script in {projectpath} and return freed space.
Run the code formatter on {projectpath} and return changed file count.
Execute the smoke test at {scriptpath} and return pass/fail.
Run the dependency audit in {projectpath} and return vulnerabilities.
Execute {codefile} with environment variables {envvars} set.
api_call: Fetch the latest records from the {api} API and return as JSON.
Call the {api} endpoint with the provided parameters.
Retrieve the current status from {api} and return the response.
Make a GET request to {endpoint} and return the response body.
Post the following payload to {endpoint}: {placeholder}
Fetch weather data for {city} from the weather API.
Call the geocoding API and return the coordinates for {city}.
Retrieve the exchange rate between USD and {currency} from the API.
Query the stock API for the current price of {ticker}.
Fetch the last 30 days of transaction data from the finance API.
Call the authentication endpoint and return the access token.
Retrieve the user profile for ID {placeholder} from the API.
Fetch paginated results from {api} starting at offset {placeholder}.
Call the search API with query {placeholder} and return top 10 results.
Retrieve the API health status and return the uptime percentage.
Make a PUT request to {endpoint} with body {placeholder} and return status.
Send a DELETE request to {endpoint} and confirm the response code.
Call the {api} webhook with the event payload and return acknowledgement.
Fetch all items from {endpoint} using cursor-based pagination.
Retrieve the OAuth2 token from {api} using client credentials flow.
Make a PATCH request to {endpoint} to update {field} and return the diff.
Poll {endpoint} every {interval} seconds until status equals {status}.
Call the {api} batch endpoint with {batchsize} items and return results.
Retrieve rate-limit headers from {endpoint} and return remaining quota.
Fetch the OpenAPI schema from {endpoint} and return the operations list.
Call the {api} metrics endpoint and return the last 24h summary.
Retrieve the list of available {resource} from {endpoint}.
Make a HEAD request to {endpoint} and return the Content-Type header.
Call the {api} export endpoint and download the CSV to {destpath}.
Fetch the changelog from {endpoint} and return the latest version.
Call the {api} streaming endpoint and collect the first {batchsize} events.
Retrieve the server time from {endpoint} and return as ISO 8601.
Make a POST request to {endpoint} with multipart form data and return ID.
Call the {api} search endpoint with filters {placeholder} and return count.
Retrieve the subscription status for account {placeholder} from {api}.
Fetch the audit log from {endpoint} for the past 7 days.
Call the {api} quota endpoint and return used versus total.
Make a GET request to {endpoint} with bearer token and return body.
Retrieve the list of supported regions from {endpoint}.
Call the {api} rollback endpoint for version {placeholder} and confirm.
mcp_tool: Use the {tool} tool to {action} and return the result.
Invoke the {tool} function with parameters: {placeholder}.
Call the {tool} tool to retrieve the {resource} for {entity}.
Use the file_reader tool to open {file} and extract {criterion}.
Invoke the database_query tool with the provided SQL and return rows.
Call list_directory on {path} and return file names.
Use the code_executor tool to run {codefile} and capture stdout.
Invoke the api_caller tool with endpoint {endpoint} and method GET.
Call the data_transformer tool on {file} and return JSON.
Use the search_tool to find {criterion} in {path}.
Invoke the summarise_document tool on {file} and return key points.
Call the schema_validator tool on {file} and return any errors.
Use the query_runner tool to execute the SQL in {file}.
Invoke the file_writer tool to save the results to {path}.
Call the metrics_collector tool and return the system stats.
Use the {tool} tool with input {placeholder} and return structured output.
Invoke the {tool} MCP function for {entity} and return the response.
Call the environment_inspector tool and return the current config.
Use the log_reader tool on {path} and return the last {linecount} lines.
Invoke the process_manager tool to list running jobs and their PIDs.
Call the secret_fetcher tool for key {placeholder} and return the value.
Use the notification_sender tool to dispatch an alert for {entity}.
Invoke the cache_manager tool to flush the cache for {resource}.
Call the health_checker tool on {endpoint} and return uptime.
Use the report_generator tool on {file} and return the PDF path.
Invoke the data_validator tool on {file} and return error counts.
Call the backup_tool to snapshot {path} and return the archive path.
Use the permission_checker tool on {path} and return access rights.
Invoke the dependency_resolver tool for {projectpath} and list packages.
Call the event_emitter tool with payload {placeholder} and confirm.
